eyebrow.es: Breakout de alfabetización en IA generativa · Grado 4
eyebrow.vi: Breakout hiểu biết AI tạo sinh · Lớp 4
eyebrow.ar: تحدّي الثقافة في الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي · الصف 4
eyebrow.ur: جنریٹِو اے آئی خواندگی بریک آؤٹ · جماعت 4
eyebrow.zh: 生成式 AI 素养破解 · 4 年级

chrome.sub: An AI helper keeps giving wrong and unfair answers. The problem isn't magic — it's the data. Crack four locks to find out why.
chrome.briefP: An AI is only as smart as the examples it learns from. We call those examples data. Read the clues, then open all four locks. Each lock has a reason that shows why the answer is right.
chrome.footerText: Gen AI Literacy Breakouts · Runs entirely in your browser — no logins, no data collected.
chrome.winP: You learned that AI learns from data, that one-sided or bad data leads to wrong and unfair guesses, that AI can make things up, and that better examples and trusted sources fix it. Great detective work!

clues.title: Bad examples, bad guesses
clues.body: An AI learns from data, which is just examples. If the examples are few, bad, or one-sided, its guesses will be bad too. Computer people say it like this: garbage in, garbage out.

clues.title: A one-sided lesson
clues.body: Someone taught an AI about apples using only pictures of red apples. Now it thinks a green apple is NOT an apple. The examples were one-sided, so the guess is wrong and unfair.

clues.title: It can invent things
clues.body: An AI wrote a book report and added a character who is not in the book at all. AI can make up things that sound true. That is why you check its work against the real source.

clues.title: How to fix it
clues.body: To fix the apple AI, you show it red, green, AND yellow apples. More examples and more KINDS of examples help it make fairer, better guesses.

clues.title: Trust the source
clues.body: When an AI gives you a fact, compare it to a source you trust, like a real book or your teacher. If the AI and the source disagree, trust the source, not the guess.

clues.title: A class event
clues.body: The class field day is next Tuesday, and the tug-of-war is everyone's favorite. This is a fun fact about the class — but it will not help you open any lock.

locks.title: Why It Was Wrong
locks.q: Why did the apple AI decide a green apple was 'not an apple'?
options: Because green apples are not really apples
Because it only learned from red-apple examples, so its data was one-sided
Because apples change color whenever a computer looks at them
locks.reason: The AI only saw red apples, so its data was one-sided. One-sided data in means wrong, unfair guesses out — garbage in, garbage out.

locks.title: Name the Examples
locks.q: The examples an AI learns from are called its ______.
locks.reason: The examples an AI learns from are called data. If the data is bad or one-sided, the AI's guesses are too.

locks.title: Make It Better
locks.q: Check EVERY thing that would make an AI's guesses BETTER and FAIRER.

items.t: Giving it more kinds of examples, not just one kind
Checking its facts against a trusted source
Fixing the mistakes in the data it learned from
Only ever showing it one kind of example

locks.reason: Better and more varied data, plus checking facts against trusted sources, makes guesses fairer. One-sided data and blind trust make them worse.

locks.title: Fix the Report
locks.q: Your AI book report has a made-up character. Tap the steps to fix it in the RIGHT order.

pads.e: 📖 Compare it to the real book

locks.reason: First NOTICE the fact that seems made up. Then COMPARE it to the real book. Then FIX the report yourself. Notice, Compare, Fix.
